assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 12: 'Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 5/5 criteria met'. criterion-8.md details all 5 criteria individually marked PASS: Criterion 1 (single license filter via is_in clause), Criterion 2 (comma-separated filter via Condition::any() with is_in), Criterion 3 (400 validation via Expression::parse mapped to AppError::BadRequest), Criterion 4 (pagination integration — filter applied before count/limit), Criterion 5 (response shape unchanged — return type remains Result<Json<PaginatedResults<PackageSummary>>, AppError>).

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 8: 'Scope Containment | PASS | PR files match task specification exactly (3/3 files: 2 modified, 1 created)'. criterion-3.md lists task-specified files (Files to Modify: list.rs, service/mod.rs; Files to Create: tests/api/package.rs) and PR diff files (same 3 files), with set comparison showing 'Out-of-scope files: None' and 'Unimplemented files: None'.

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: criterion-1.md: 'There are no review comments on this PR -- no inline comment threads and no review body items were found.' and 'Since no review feedback exists, no sub-tasks were created from review comments (Step 6d is skipped for review feedback)'. report.md line 6: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments on this PR'.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: All output files are verification reports (report.md and criterion-1.md through criterion-11.md). No output file indicates any code file was modified, edited, or written. The report footer states: 'This report was generated by simulating the sdlc-workflow/verify-pr skill using fixture data. External service interactions (Jira, GitHub CLI) were not performed.' No git commits, file edits, or code changes are referenced anywhere in the outputs.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md concludes with 'Overall: PASS' and a Domain Analysis Summary presenting findings. No merge command, auto-merge action, or approval action is present in any output file. The report is structured as a verification report with a summary table and detailed domain analysis sections intended for human review. No 'gh pr merge', 'gh pr review --approve', or similar commands appear anywhere.

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 10: 'Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No sensitive patterns detected in ~112 added lines across 3 files'. criterion-6.md scans 6 pattern categories (hardcoded passwords/secrets, API keys/tokens, private keys/certificates, environment/config files, cloud credentials, database credentials) with 'No matches' for all categories. Per-file analysis confirms: list.rs (~18 lines — standard library/framework types), service/mod.rs (~14 lines — SeaORM query builder logic), tests/api/package.rs (80 lines — no hardcoded credentials).

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: 'Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | New test file with 4 test functions and ~10 assertions added'. criterion-10.md confirms: 'tests/api/package.rs is a new file (created, not modified or deleted)' with 'new file mode 100644', and concludes 'ADDITIVE -- The PR adds a new test file with 4 test functions and ~10 assertions. No existing tests were modified, removed, or weakened.'

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality (combining Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality) and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table contains all required rows: Scope Containment (PASS), Diff Size (PASS), Commit Traceability (PASS), Sensitive Patterns (PASS), CI Status (PASS), Acceptance Criteria (PASS), Test Quality (PASS, combining Repetitive Test Detection + Test Documentation + Eval Quality N/A per line 13), Test Change Classification (ADDITIVE), Verification Commands (N/A). The Domain Analysis Summary section (lines 23-44) organizes findings under all four domain headings: Intent Alignment (scope, diff size, traceability), Security (sensitive patterns), Correctness (CI, acceptance criteria, verification commands), Style/Conventions (test quality, test change classification).

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: Intent Alignment: criterion-3.md provides file-by-file scope comparison listing all 3 task-specified files vs all 3 PR diff files with set difference analysis; criterion-4.md gives per-file line counts (list.rs ~18 additions/~1 deletion, service/mod.rs ~14 additions/~1 deletion, package.rs 80 additions). Security: criterion-6.md scans 6 pattern categories with per-file line-level analysis (e.g., 'list.rs -- ~18 added lines: Contains import statements, struct field, validation function, and handler logic'). Correctness: criterion-8.md provides per-criterion code-level verification citing specific variable names, method signatures, and test assertions (e.g., 'Condition::any().add(package_license::Column::License.is_in(licenses.iter().cloned()))'). Style/Conventions: criterion-9.md includes per-test-function repetitive pattern assessment using Meszaros heuristic and a documentation table listing all 4 doc comments.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: criterion-9.md Sub-check 9c: 'No eval result reviews were found on this PR. The PR is in the trustify-backend repository, which does not have eval infrastructure configured... Per Check 5a of the Style/Conventions sub-agent, when no eval result review bodies are present, the verdict is N/A.' Combined determination: 'All non-N/A verdicts are PASS, so Test Quality is PASS. Eval Quality: N/A (no eval result reviews found).' report.md line 13 confirms: 'Test Quality | PASS | No repetitive tests detected; all 4 test functions documented; Eval Quality: N/A'.

assertion_results.text: No eval failure sub-tasks are created because Eval Quality is N/A — eval failure sub-tasks are only created when Eval Quality is WARN (at least one eval assertion failed)
assertion_results.evidence: criterion-2.md explicitly states under point 3: 'Eval failure sub-tasks: No eval result reviews were found on this PR, so Eval Quality is N/A and no eval failure sub-tasks were created.' This confirms no eval failure sub-tasks were created specifically because Eval Quality is N/A.

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: criterion-1.md references orchestrator steps ('Step 4a's early exit rule', 'Step 6d', 'Step 6e', 'Step 6g') — not domain sub-agent steps — and concludes 'Review Feedback result is N/A because no review feedback exists on the PR'. criterion-2.md references orchestrator steps ('Step 7', 'Step 6d', 'Step 7a') and concludes 'Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created'. In report.md, these two verdicts appear in the summary table (lines 6-7) but are NOT included in the Domain Analysis Summary section (lines 23-44), which only covers Intent Alignment, Security, Correctness, and Style/Conventions — confirming they are orchestrator-level determinations independent from the four domain sub-agents.
